project.name: HarvestHub
project.tagline: Adaptive product scraping platform with premium exports
project.git: Not initialized (not a git repo yet)

handoff.description:
A TypeScript-first web scraping platform that uses Python's Scrapling library for adaptive, anti-bot product data extraction from ANY e-commerce site.
Architecture: Hybrid TypeScript + Python - TypeScript handles: CLI, rate limiting, retries, normalization, storage, export, API, dashboard - Python handles: actual HTTP fetching + HTML parsing via Scrapling (subprocess JSON IPC)
The platform extracts product data (title, price, description, images, availability, brand, SKU, MPN, GTIN, category, rating, reviews, specifications) using a 3-tier extraction strategy: JSON-LD → Microdata → CSS heuristics, each with confidence scoring.
Exports to: XLSX (premium styled), CSV, JSON, Google Merchant Center TSV feed.
Includes a premium SaaS-style dashboard (dark theme, charts, search, filters, pagination) and a premium editorial documentation/landing page.

completed.item: Python Scrapling engine with 3-tier adaptive extraction
CLI with 5 commands: scrape, export, status, migrate, dashboard
4 export formats: XLSX, CSV, JSON, GMC
Retry engine (exponential backoff + jitter + error classification)
User agent rotation (20 real browser UAs)
Legacy migration from export_all.py (101 products imported)
Live scraping tested and working (3/3 books.toscrape.com URLs)
104 products in data store (101 migrated + 3 live scraped)
Express.js API server (5 endpoints) at port 4000
Python dependencies installed (scrapling, curl_cffi, orjson, browserforge)

potential_improvements.item: Delete AI-Training-Story-EN-DE.html (user said to discard it)
Job scheduling with cron (croner package already installed)
Result diffing (detect price changes over time)

technical_knowledge.critical_bug_fix:
Scrapling v0.4.1's Response object has css(selector) which returns a LIST, not a single element. There is NO css_first() method on the Response object. We created a helper function in engine/scraper.py:
def css_first(page, selector): results = page.css(selector) return results[0] if results else None
This is used in ALL 14+ places where we query the DOM. Elements have .text (string) and .attrib (dict) for attribute access. Response also has .status, .headers, .cookies, .url, .html_content.

Scrapling prints deprecation warnings and INFO logs to stderr/stdout. These corrupt the JSON IPC protocol (TypeScript reads stdout expecting pure JSON). Fix at top of engine/scraper.py: import warnings import logging warnings.filterwarnings("ignore") logging.disable(logging.CRITICAL)

Scrapling's Fetcher class requires the 'browserforge' package at runtime. Without it, scraping fails with ImportError. Install: pip install browserforge (This is NOT listed in Scrapling's requirements, discovered empirically)
technical_knowledge.known_issue: Port 3000 is already in use on the user's machine. Dashboard server runs on port 4000 instead. Command: npx tsx src/cli/index.ts dashboard -p 4000
When taking screenshots with Playwright, wait_until='networkidle' hangs indefinitely because Google Fonts connections never fully resolve. Use wait_until='commit' + wait_for_timeout(2500) instead.

handoff.file_tree: h:\scraper\ ├── .gitignore ├── package.json ├── package-lock.json ├── tsconfig.json ├── setup.ps1 ├── README.md ├── export_all.py (legacy, preserved) ├── AI-Training-Story-EN-DE.html (TO BE DELETED) ├── sample-urls.txt ├── test-urls.txt ├── engine/ │ ├── scraper.py (Python Scrapling engine, 380 lines) │ └── requirements.txt (scrapling>=0.2, orjson>=3.9) ├── src/ │ ├── types/ │ │ ├── index.ts (re-exports) │ │ ├── product.ts (Product, ConfidenceScores, Availability) │ │ ├── job.ts (ScrapeJob, ScrapeTask, JobConfig) │ │ └── schedule.ts (Schedule) │ ├── lib/ │ │ ├── errors.ts (HarvestError hierarchy) │ │ ├── logger.ts (Pino logger) │ │ ├── url-parser.ts (parse URL files/strings) │ │ └── url-validator.ts (validate + deduplicate) │ ├── core/ │ │ ├── scrape-bridge.ts (TypeScript↔Python subprocess IPC) │ │ ├── rate-limiter.ts (per-domain token bucket) │ │ ├── retry-engine.ts (exponential backoff + jitter) │ │ └── ua-pool.ts (20 user agents, round-robin) │ ├── pipeline/ │ │ └── normalizer.ts (price, currency, availability normalization) │ ├── store/ │ │ └── local-store.ts (JSON file persistence, atomic writes) │ ├── export/ │ │ ├── index.ts (format router) │ │ ├── xlsx-exporter.ts (ExcelJS, premium styling) │ │ ├── csv-exporter.ts (UTF-8 BOM) │ │ ├── json-exporter.ts (structured with metadata) │ │ └── gmc-exporter.ts (Google Merchant Center TSV) │ ├── api/ │ │ ├── server.ts (Express.js, 5 endpoints + static) │ │ └── start.ts (standalone entry) │ ├── cli/ │ │ ├── index.ts (Commander.js entry, 5 commands) │ │ └── commands/ │ │ ├── scrape.ts (main scrape orchestrator) │ │ ├── export.ts (export stored products) │ │ ├── status.ts (system status) │ │ ├── migrate.ts (legacy data import) │ │ └── dashboard.ts (start web dashboard) │ └── __tests__/ │ ├── errors.test.ts │ ├── local-store.test.ts │ ├── normalizer.test.ts │ ├── ua-pool.test.ts │ └── url-validator.test.ts ├── dashboard/ │ ├── index.html (SaaS dashboard, 30KB) │ └── docs.html (documentation landing page, 51KB) └── data/ ├── store/ │ └── default.json (104 products) ├── exports/ (generated files) └── logs/

commands.command: npx tsx src/cli/index.ts export -f xlsx -o data/exports/products.xlsx
npx tsx src/cli/index.ts export -f gmc -o data/exports/feed.tsv

dependencies.production: chalk@^5.3.0, cli-progress@^3.12.0, commander@^12.1.0, croner@^8.0.0, exceljs@^4.4.0, express@^4.18.2, nanoid@^5.0.7, ora@^8.0.1, pino@^9.0.0, pino-pretty@^11.0.0, ws@^8.16.0, zod@^3.23.0
dependencies.python: scrapling>=0.2, orjson>=3.9 (also installed: curl_cffi, browserforge — runtime deps of scrapling)

api.endpoint: System status, engine health, product count
Paginated products with search/filter (query: page, limit, search, brand, availability)
Aggregated stats: totals, averages, top brands/categories
Download export file (format: xlsx, csv, json, gmc)

user_context.goal: Make the scraping project so impressive the user's boss doesn't fire them
user_context.budget: Zero — no paid APIs, proxies, or services
user_context.audience: The user's boss — expects a clean, premium, SaaS-quality product
user_context.language_preference: TypeScript for everything, Python only for Scrapling engine
user_context.discarded_file: AI-Training-Story-EN-DE.html — user said to discard entirely

handoff.instructions:
You are picking up an in-progress project called HarvestHub at h:\scraper. The project is a TypeScript+Python hybrid web scraping platform. Everything described in the "completed" section above is already built and working.
BEFORE making any changes: 1. Run "npx vitest run" to confirm 48/48 tests still pass 2. Run "npx tsc --noEmit" to confirm 0 TypeScript errors 3. Check the dashboard works: "npx tsx src/cli/index.ts dashboard -p 4000"
CRITICAL REMINDERS: - Scrapling has NO css_first() method. Always use the css_first() helper in engine/scraper.py - Scrapling warnings MUST be suppressed or they corrupt JSON IPC - browserforge is a required but undocumented Scrapling dependency - Port 3000 is occupied; use port 4000 - Working directory is h:\scraper on Windows - All file paths use backslashes on this system
The user wants this to be AMAZING — premium quality, SaaS-grade. Ask the user what they'd like to improve or add next.
